status: BLOCKED — trigger defect surfaced; flag rolled back to false

post_rollback_state.rollback_reason: Step F surfaced a trigger defect that would actively break live cycle-count UPDATEs while shadow_write=true. See defect_blocking_canary.

step_b_pre_backfill_snapshot.interpretation: Expected starting state — projection has never run because shadow_write was off until Step A. The drift view sees missing_in_shadow=8908 because every legacy row currently lacks a work_tasks projection. The other three drift dimensions (assignee/priority/status) are 0 since they are only computed over the intersection of rows present in both tables and that intersection is empty.

step_c_backfill.reason_not_run: (a) Service role key not present in repo .env files (only frontend anon key in .env.local); (b) trigger defect would have caused the script to fail on every chunk anyway — see defect_blocking_canary.
step_c_backfill.script_design_note: The script bumps `updated_at` on each chunk to fire the AFTER UPDATE projection trigger. Inspection of the trigger function (sync_cycle_count_to_work_task) shows the UPDATE branch only updates *existing* work_tasks rows — for missing projections (which is the entire 8908-row backlog), the UPDATE matches zero rows and is a silent no-op. To project historical rows the trigger UPDATE branch would need to be changed to an UPSERT (INSERT … ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE), or the backfill would need to issue a direct INSERT … SELECT mirroring the trigger's INSERT branch logic. This is a follow-up beyond the scope of this Phase 11.1 task. Surfacing it in this baseline so the operator does not run the script blindly and assume the silent no-op success means projection succeeded.

step_d_drift_verification.verdict: BLOCKED — backfill did not run, defect must be patched first. After patch the operator should re-run Steps A–G and expect missing_in_shadow=0 (or whatever number reflects the post-backfill state).

step_e_spot_check.reason: No projection rows exist (work_tasks_total=0). LEFT JOIN to work_tasks would return wt.* = NULL for all three picks, which is not a meaningful match check.

step_f_sync_loop_test.scenario: While work_tasks_shadow_write=true, ran `UPDATE rr_cyclecount_data SET notes = COALESCE(notes,'') || ' [shadow test]' WHERE id = 'e7532d59-54e5-466c-95ce-984f2854268d'`.
step_f_sync_loop_test.result: FAILED with PG error 42804 (COALESCE types cycle_count_priority and text cannot be matched).
step_f_sync_loop_test.interpretation: The UPDATE rolled back atomically (notes column unchanged on retry). work_events_count = 0 before and after — but only because the trigger's whole UPDATE branch threw and the outer UPDATE was rolled back. So this is NOT a clean PASS for the no-loop check; it's a hard FAIL on a deeper class of bug — the trigger cannot run UPDATEs at all under the current schema mismatch.
step_f_sync_loop_test.verdict: BLOCKED — trigger function defect is a precondition to even running the loop test.

defect_blocking_canary.name: Trigger function `sync_cycle_count_to_work_task` UPDATE branch cannot resolve COALESCE between cycle_count_priority enum and text.
defect_blocking_canary.severity: P0 — cannot enable shadow_write for any org without breaking all live UPDATEs on rr_cyclecount_data for that org.
defect_blocking_canary.scope: Forward-projection trigger (rr_cyclecount_data → work_tasks) AFTER UPDATE branch only. INSERT branch uses `COALESCE(NEW.priority, 'normal')` which resolves through the enum input function and works via the implicit enum→text assignment cast on insert.
defect_blocking_canary.exact_pg_error: ERROR:  42804: COALESCE types cycle_count_priority and text cannot be matched
defect_blocking_canary.how_it_was_missed: Migration 261's `work_engine_drift` view had the same class of bug and was patched in Pass 3 with `rcc.priority::text` (per session 2026-05-02 addendum). Migration 257's trigger function was *not* patched at the same time because it had not yet been exercised — `work_tasks_shadow_write` was false everywhere, so no trigger run had ever attempted the COALESCE.
defect_blocking_canary.recommended_fix: In migration 257 (or a follow-up migration), CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.sync_cycle_count_to_work_task() with the UPDATE branch line changed to `priority = COALESCE(NEW.priority::text, priority),`. Defensively also cast `NEW.status::text` and `NEW.count_type::text` and `NEW.push_mode::text` if they end up being any non-text type, though only priority is strictly required to unblock. Mirror the same change in `sync_work_task_to_cycle_count` reverse trigger if it has analogous text→enum assignments (the reverse trigger sets `status = v_legacy_status` where v_legacy_status is text and rr_cyclecount_data.status is the enum — that direction *does* have an implicit text→enum assignment cast for enums whose label set contains the runtime value, so it would work for the documented mapping; but worth a follow-on hardening pass.).
secondary_followups_unblocked_by_fix: Backfill mechanics: the script's UPDATE-fires-trigger model only refreshes existing projections. Historical rows need an UPSERT branch in the trigger or a direct INSERT … SELECT seeding pass before the script becomes useful for first-time backfill of an org. See step_c_backfill.script_design_note.
Reverse trigger sync_work_task_to_cycle_count needs a parallel review for any text→enum assignments before reads cut over.

next_operator_actions: 1. Patch migration 257's `sync_cycle_count_to_work_task` function (or write a follow-up migration 264_patch_cycle_count_projection_priority_cast.sql) that does a CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION with the UPDATE branch's `priority = COALESCE(NEW.priority::text, priority),` cast.

2. While at it, also fix the backfill mechanics: change the trigger UPDATE branch to an UPSERT (INSERT … ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE) so that the chunked driver's `bump updated_at` call actually projects historical rows.
3. Re-run the canary kickoff (Steps A–G in this task) against j.AI OneBox after the patch lands.
4. Confirm the same pattern review covers reverse trigger sync_work_task_to_cycle_count (text→enum assignments).
5. Only after a clean Steps A–G pass with drift=0, hold the 8h soak window before flipping work_tasks_read_shadow per Phase 11.3.

265_found_part_transfer_projection_safet.scope: (A) NEW.priority::text cast in projection trigger; (B) UPDATE branch → INSERT…ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE upsert; (C) reverse trigger writes status::cycle_count_status defensively; (D) count_type='found_part_transfer' exemption (forced requires_recount=false, clamped variance_review→completed/approved)

New trigger trg_zzz_enforce_work_task_zone_exclusivity fires AFTER all maintenance triggers; honors both legacy (app.cycle_count_zone_lock_bypass) and new (app.work_zone_lock_bypass) GUC bypass; raises distinct ZONE_ASSIGNED / 'ZONE_LOCKED: active' / 'ZONE_LOCKED: reserved' errors.

migration_ledger_check.ordering_observation: 264 was authored against the prior 263-tip baseline and was applied before 266 even though the filename ordinal is lower than 265's. PostgreSQL has no constraint against this; the schema state is identical to the linear case because all three migrations are additive and disjoint.

step_d_drift_verification.verdict: PASS — every field-level drift dimension stays at 0 throughout the run; missing_in_shadow tracks 1:1 with the number of legacy rows we have not yet projected. Gate to Phase 11.3 is field-level drift = 0 over the projected set, which is satisfied.

step_f_sync_loop_test.scenario: While work_tasks_shadow_write=true, ran `UPDATE rr_cyclecount_data SET notes = COALESCE(notes,'') || ' [shadow test]' WHERE id = 'e7532d59-54e5-466c-95ce-984f2854268d'`.
step_f_sync_loop_test.result: SUCCESS — UPDATE returned the row with notes appended (no 42804). Trigger fired exactly once; UPSERT branch updated the existing work_tasks projection's result_payload.notes to match the new legacy notes value.
step_f_sync_loop_test.interpretation: Δ=0 confirms (a) no infinite loop between forward and reverse projection (the app.skip_sync GUC short-circuits the reverse trigger after the forward trigger sets it), and (b) the legacy UPDATE path does not write a work_events row directly — only dispatcher RPCs do. The projection's result_payload was observed mid-test as `[Part Verified] 23078036 confirmed at location. [shadow test]` (mirroring the legacy notes), then reverted along with the legacy notes column.
step_f_sync_loop_test.test_note_revert: Confirmed both legacy and projection notes back to `[Part Verified] 23078036 confirmed at location.` (pre-task content).

next_operator_actions: 1. Begin the Phase 11.3 8h soak watch on j.AI OneBox. Periodically poll work_engine_drift; alert on any (assignee_drift|priority_drift|status_drift) > 0 — those are the gating signals.

2. Drive ≥ 50 cycle-count lifecycle transitions during the window covering pull-claim, supervisor push, complete, release, photo/artifact, and signoff paths (per phase-11-rollout.md §11.3). If real volume is low, run the staging replay script (operator-driven; not in repo).

3. Optionally schedule the full 8908-row backfill via the documented operator command above to drive missing_in_shadow → 0 before read_shadow. Drift dashboard treats missing_in_shadow as the primary gate for the read flip; field drifts must remain 0.
4. After the soak window completes cleanly, flip work_tasks_read_shadow=true (NOT in this task's scope).

5. Phase 11.4a pre-read_primary checklist (in docs/work-engine/phase-11-rollout.md) still applies — the four deferred items (found-part-transfer projection branch, work_tasks zone exclusivity ✓ now done via mig 266, strategy registry → claim_next wiring, database types regen) need to land before flipping work_tasks_read_primary.
